rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Suspicious Process Lineage - WMI

rules.rule_text:
title: Suspicious Process Lineage - WMI
id: 75e6bb57-d135-4308-b0ac-936e2d76d4d5
status: experimental
description: "In general, trusted binaries and known administrative tools and processes\
  \ will initiate WMI \nactivity. As such, it makes sense to look for known bad processes\
  \ launching WMI or deviations \nfrom the expected where a legitimate but unusual\
  \ Windows binary spawns WMI\u2014or spawns from it. \nThe following is an amalgamation\
  \ of several analytics that can detect a wide array of threats, \nranging from red\
  \ team activity to web shells to coinminers. Part of the RedCanary 2024 Threat \n\
  Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-management-instrumentation/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1047
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: \wmiprvse.exe
    Image|endswith:
    - \rundll32.exe
    - \msbuild.exe
    - \powershell.exe
    - \cmd.exe
    - \mshta.exe
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
